important_points: The U.S.
Department of Agriculture confirmed a second case of New World screwworm, a flesh-eating parasite, in Texas.

items.title: Chinese EVs may hit U.S. within a few years, one way or another
items.title_zh: Chinese EVs may hit U.S. within a few years, one way or another
items.summary: 未调用大模型，以下为原文关键信息摘录：Chinese electric vehicles face crippling tariffs, stringent regulations and fierce opposition from lawmakers and the American auto industry, but there's a growing possibility that Chinese EVs will be sold in the U.S.；within the next few years.；China has deliberately and aggressively expanded its EV footprint throughout Europe, the U.K., Asia and Australia, exporting millions of well designed, high-tech, and competitively priced vehicles, building factories and widening supply chains.
important_points: Chinese electric vehicles face crippling tariffs, stringent regulations and fierce opposition from lawmakers and the American auto industry, but there's a growing possibility that Chinese EVs will be sold in the U.S.
within the next few years.

China has deliberately and aggressively expanded its EV footprint throughout Europe, the U.K., Asia and Australia, exporting millions of well designed, high-tech, and competitively priced vehicles, building factories and widening supply chains.

items.title: Remote work career risks for Gen Z are 'subtle, but material,' Harvard professor says: How to avoid the pitfalls
items.title_zh: 对Z世代远程工作的职业风险‘微小但实在’，教授如何避开陷阱
items.summary: 未调用大模型，以下为原文关键信息摘录：While remote work offers young people flexibility, autonomy and a commute-free day, research shows that forgoing the office can have a detrimental impact on those at the start of their careers.；Remote work is likely the main cause of a surge in youth unemployment, a report this month by the Federal Reserve Bank of New York found.；The National Bureau of Economic Research has also found, in a paper updated this month, that the rise in remote work makes it harder for younger workers to get feedback and advance.
important_points: While remote work offers young people flexibility, autonomy and a commute-free day, research shows that forgoing the office can have a detrimental impact on those at the start of their careers.
Remote work is likely the main cause of a surge in youth unemployment, a report this month by the Federal Reserve Bank of New York found.
The National Bureau of Economic Research has also found, in a paper updated this month, that the rise in remote work makes it harder for younger workers to get feedback and advance.

items.title: Wall Street's 'fear gauge' punches back as the 'crash up' in chip stocks finally reverses
items.title_zh: 华尔街恐慌指数回升，芯片股跌幅反弹
items.summary: 近期股市波动剧烈，但Cboe波动指数显示市场情绪趋稳。芯片板块连续80%上涨，推动纳斯达克100指数市值增长半万亿美元，ETF推出成功，单股剧烈波动。市场参与者认为这一波动反映了IPO发行压力和利率上升的预警。
important_points: 芯片股连续上涨，市值增长显著
VIX跌破长期低点，单日跌幅大
IPO发行压力和利率上升被视为警示信号

items.title: Abel goes his own way with new Berkshire investments, including billions for AI
items.title_zh: Abel goes his own way with new Berkshire investments, including billions for AI
items.summary: 未调用大模型，以下为原文关键信息摘录：(This is the Warren Buffett Watch newsletter, news and analysis on all things Warren Buffett and Berkshire Hathaway.；You can sign up here to receive it every Friday evening in your inbox.) Buffett praises new CEO for 'fast' and 'smooth' acquisition Warren Buffett tells CNBC's Becky Quick new Berkshire Hathaway CEO Greg Abel has "launched" with his first major deal, the $6.8 billion acquisition of Taylor Morrison Home, a residential homebuilder and developer with operations in 12 states.；On Monday's "Squawk Box," Becky quoted Buffett from a phone conversation the day before when the deal was announced: "Greg did this faster than I could have done it, smoother than I could have done it, and I never talked to the CEO.
important_points: (This is the Warren Buffett Watch newsletter, news and analysis on all things Warren Buffett and Berkshire Hathaway.

You can sign up here to receive it every Friday evening in your inbox.) Buffett praises new CEO for 'fast' and 'smooth' acquisition Warren Buffett tells CNBC's Becky Quick new Berkshire Hathaway CEO Greg Abel has "launched" with his first major deal, the $6.8 billion acquisition of Taylor Morrison Home, a residential homebuilder and developer with operations in 12 states.

On Monday's "Squawk Box," Becky quoted Buffett from a phone conversation the day before when the deal was announced: "Greg did this faster than I could have done it, smoother than I could have done it, and I never talked to the CEO.

items.title: Inside the Obama-era Iran nuclear deal that Trump withdrew from
items.title_zh: Inside the Obama-era Iran nuclear deal that Trump withdrew from
items.summary: 未调用大模型，以下为原文关键信息摘录：President Donald Trump on Friday defended the continued lack of a war-ending deal with Iran by once again trashing the prior nuclear agreement brokered by Barack Obama, his predecessor and longtime political foe.；"They've dealt with very weak and ineffective leadership on behalf of the United States" and others "that allowed them to get away with murder," Trump said of Iran in an NBC News interview.；He was asked why Iran is still holding out in negotiations if they are desperate to reach an agreement, as Trump insists that they are.
important_points: President Donald Trump on Friday defended the continued lack of a war-ending deal with Iran by once again trashing the prior nuclear agreement brokered by Barack Obama, his predecessor and longtime political foe.
"They've dealt with very weak and ineffective leadership on behalf of the United States" and others "that allowed them to get away with murder," Trump said of Iran in an NBC News interview.
He was asked why Iran is still holding out in negotiations if they are desperate to reach an agreement, as Trump insists that they are.

items.title: Oracle: Next Level Up (NYSE:ORCL)
items.title_zh: Oracle 进入季四盈利，市场看好
items.summary: Oracle 公司正进入季四，财务表现更为强劲，预期IPO成功。公司通过扩展数据中心容量，客户资金充裕，财务风险降低。分析师预测多年收入目标实现，股价近10倍EPS目标，投资潜力大。
important_points: 季四业绩提升，支持IPO计划
客户资金充裕，资本压力减轻
成本控制措施带来EPS提升
股价接近目标，市场预期积极

items.title: Social media bans on teens risk strengthening Big Tech's grip on the sector, Bluesky exec warns
items.title_zh: Social media bans on teens risk strengthening Big Tech's grip on the sector, Bluesky exec warns
items.summary: 未调用大模型，以下为原文关键信息摘录：Government action to ban social media platforms risks strengthening Big Tech's grip on the industry further, limiting access for smaller players, an exec at BlueSky has warned.；Rose Wang, Bluesky's chief operating officer, told CNBC on the sidelines of SXSW in London on Wednesday that the smaller open-source platform isn't opposed to regulation but that smaller players in the industry should be protected.；"I support the protection and the safety of youth, the question that we have then is at what cost, because essentially what I'm scared of is in the long term, we're headed to a world where there's about three to five platforms, and extreme heavy regulation of those platforms, and basically the whole compliance teams of these platforms are 10 times the size of our entire team," Wang said.
important_points: Government action to ban social media platforms risks strengthening Big Tech's grip on the industry further, limiting access for smaller players, an exec at BlueSky has warned.

Rose Wang, Bluesky's chief operating officer, told CNBC on the sidelines of SXSW in London on Wednesday that the smaller open-source platform isn't opposed to regulation but that smaller players in the industry should be protected.

"I support the protection and the safety of youth, the question that we have then is at what cost, because essentially what I'm scared of is in the long term, we're headed to a world where there's about three to five platforms, and extreme heavy regulation of those platforms, and basically the whole compliance teams of these platforms are 10 times the size of our entire team," Wang said.

items.title: AstraZeneca CEO says AI is reshaping drug development — and helping boost the odds of success
items.title_zh: AstraZeneca CEO says AI is reshaping drug development — and helping boost the odds of success
items.summary: 未调用大模型，以下为原文关键信息摘录：AstraZeneca CEO Pascal Soriot said artificial intelligence is helping drugmakers develop medicines faster and make smarter decisions throughout the research process.；"The value of AI in our industry is productivity improvement," Soriot said on CNBC's "Mad Money" on Friday.；"In the way you design a new medicine, a new drug, you can actually do it faster, do it smarter." The comments come as investors increasingly question whether the massive spending on artificial intelligence is producing meaningful returns in industries like healthcare.
important_points: AstraZeneca CEO Pascal Soriot said artificial intelligence is helping drugmakers develop medicines faster and make smarter decisions throughout the research process.
"The value of AI in our industry is productivity improvement," Soriot said on CNBC's "Mad Money" on Friday.

"In the way you design a new medicine, a new drug, you can actually do it faster, do it smarter." The comments come as investors increasingly question whether the massive spending on artificial intelligence is producing meaningful returns in industries like healthcare.

items.title: Trump administration, OpenAI discussing possible government stake in the AI startup
items.title_zh: Trump administration, OpenAI discussing possible government stake in the AI startup
items.summary: 未调用大模型，以下为原文关键信息摘录：OpenAI CEO Sam Altman and the White House are in ongoing talks about a possible government stake in the artificial intelligence company, CNBC confirmed on Friday.；The discussions have been in progress for more than a year, as Altman first shared the idea with the Trump administration in 2025, according to a source familiar with the matter who asked not to be named because the details are confidential.；The talks continued this week as Altman met with a range of lawmakers and officials in Washington about regulation and the latest developments in AI.
important_points: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman and the White House are in ongoing talks about a possible government stake in the artificial intelligence company, CNBC confirmed on Friday.

The discussions have been in progress for more than a year, as Altman first shared the idea with the Trump administration in 2025, according to a source familiar with the matter who asked not to be named because the details are confidential.
The talks continued this week as Altman met with a range of lawmakers and officials in Washington about regulation and the latest developments in AI.
